Title: Transcriptomic network analyses shed light on the regulation of cuticle development in maize leaves
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: W22 for mutant analysis; B73 for transcriptome/network analysis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript detected in epidermis of emerging adult leaf 8; enriched in light-exposed distal regions/module H
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Negatively correlated with immature-cuticle hydrocarbons and positively correlated with mature cuticle components in expanding leaf 8 epidermis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Inferred regulation of cuticle biosynthesis/deposition program; specific downstream genes not directly validated
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Cuticular wax composition/deposition during cuticle development
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Cuticular wax alkanes; free fatty acids
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Lipid polyester-associated surface lipid; wax/fatty acid
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf cuticle of adult maize leaves
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hydrophobic barrier reducing nonstomatal water loss/desiccation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PHYB-mediated signaling represses accumulation of specific cuticle components; phyb1 phyb2 double mutants show increased total alkanes and total free fatty acids
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major hub in module H and genetically validated regulator of wax composition
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf emergence from darkness to light along proximodistal gradient
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCN hub analysis; correlation with cuticle lipids; reverse genetics using phyb1 phyb2 double mutants; GC-MS wax profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PHYB1 was identified as a major hub in light-responsive module H enriched for cuticle genes. 2. PHYB1 transcript levels positively correlate with longer-chain mature wax components and negatively with shorter-chain immature hydrocarbons. 3. phyb1 phyb2 double mutants accumulate more total alkanes and free fatty acids, supporting a regulatory role in cuticle composition.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for engineering adult leaf cuticle composition and potentially water-loss traits; likely requires consideration of pleiotropic light signaling effects
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single phyb1 mutants showed no consistent phenotype, indicating redundancy with PHYB2

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Not detected in epidermis of emerging maize leaf 8; identified in internal tissues
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Inferred together with PHYB1 in cuticle regulatory program; direct targets not validated
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Cuticular wax alkanes; free fatty acids
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf cuticle of adult maize leaves
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts redundantly with PHYB1; loss in phyb1 phyb2 double mutants increases total alkanes and free fatty acids
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential redundant partner required to reveal PHYB-dependent wax phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf emergence from darkness to light
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double-mutant reverse genetics; GC-MS wax profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PHYB2 was not present in the epidermal WGCN because its transcripts were detected in internal tissues. 2. Genetic redundancy with PHYB1 is supported by lack of consistent phyb1 single-mutant phenotype and strong double-mutant wax phenotype. 3. phyb1 phyb2 double mutants overaccumulate alkanes and free fatty acids.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant for manipulating cuticle composition, but likely with broad photomorphogenic pleiotropy
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as core gene because double-mutant phenotyping directly tested its role in cuticle composition

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: B73 for transcriptome/network analysis; W22 for mutant analysis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in epidermis; major hub in module H; accumulation higher in light-exposed distal intervals
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Positively correlated with wax esters and mature cuticle components; negatively correlated with immature hydrocarbons
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Specific downstream target genes not directly validated
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Wax esters; chain-length-specific alcohols, fatty acids, and alkanes
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf cuticle of adult maize leaves
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PHYA function regulates cuticle composition; phya1 phya2 double mutants overaccumulate total wax esters and alter chain-length distribution of alcohols, fatty acids, and alkanes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major hub/correlated regulator supported by mutant wax phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf emergence from darkness to light
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCN hub analysis; expression-lipid correlation; phya1 phya2 double-mutant GC-MS profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PHYA1 is a major hub in module H, a light-responsive cuticle-associated coexpression module. 2. PHYA1 expression correlates positively with mature cuticle metabolites including wax esters. 3. phya1 phya2 double mutants show significant overaccumulation of total wax esters and chain-length redistribution in multiple wax classes.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential handle for tuning wax ester composition, but likely constrained by broader light-response functions
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only one phya1 phya2 double-mutant allele combination was available, so further validation was suggested by authors

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: B73 for transcriptome/network analysis; W22 for mutant analysis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in epidermis; overlapping transcript pattern with PHYA1; fewer edges than PHYA1 in module H
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Correlates with wax esters and mature cuticle components
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Wax esters; chain-length-specific alcohols, fatty acids, and alkanes
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf cuticle of adult maize leaves
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts redundantly with PHYA1 in regulating wax composition; double mutants overaccumulate total wax esters and alter chain-length distribution
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Redundant PHYA component genetically linked to wax composition
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf emergence from darkness to light
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression-lipid correlation; phya1 phya2 double-mutant GC-MS profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PHYA2 shows overlapping transcript accumulation with PHYA1 in the epidermis. 2. It is part of the light-responsive module H linked to mature cuticle features. 3. Loss together with PHYA1 alters wax ester accumulation and wax chain-length profiles.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for wax profile engineering, with caution due to broad phytochrome functions
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No prior obvious morphological phenotype reported for phya mutants, but biochemical wax phenotype was detected

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Cuticular wax accumulation in moss gametophores
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: phy2 mutants show reduced total fatty alcohols and aldehydes compared with wild type
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supports ancestral role of phytochromes in cuticle regulation across land plants
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Reverse genetics in moss; GC-MS wax profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Moss phy2 mutants display altered cuticle wax composition. 2. Total fatty alcohols and aldehydes are reduced in phy2 colonies. 3. These data support a conserved phytochrome role in land-plant cuticle regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primarily evolutionary insight rather than direct crop deployment
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Moss PHY genes are evolutionarily distinct from angiosperm PHYs

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Cuticular wax accumulation in moss gametophores
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Fatty alcohols; aldehydes; alkanes; wax esters
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: phy3 mutants show reduced total fatty alcohols, aldehydes, alkanes, and wax esters
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong moss regulator of multiple wax classes supporting ancestral phy role
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Reverse genetics in moss; GC-MS wax profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. phy3 mutants have broad reductions in several moss wax classes. 2. Total fatty alcohols, aldehydes, alkanes, and wax esters are decreased. 3. phy3 cuticles are deficient in longer-chain aldehydes.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Evolutionary relevance; not a direct crop engineering target in this study

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Cuticular wax accumulation in moss gametophores
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Fatty alcohols; aldehydes; alkanes; alicyclics; total waxes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: phy4 mutants show reduced fatty alcohols, aldehydes, alkanes, alicyclics, and total waxes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong evidence for phy-dependent regulation of moss cuticle wax accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Reverse genetics in moss; GC-MS wax profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. phy4 mutants show reductions in multiple wax classes including total waxes. 2. Total fatty alcohols, aldehydes, alkanes, and alicyclics are reduced. 3. phy4 mutants are depleted in shorter-chain aldehyde components.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Evolutionary relevance; not a direct crop target here
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phenotype differs from maize phy mutants, consistent with lineage-specific PHY diversification
